summary: Tested i18n translations and mobile responsiveness. Full-app translations work correctly on Dashboard, Teams, and Users pages for English, Portuguese, and Spanish. Mobile responsiveness works on all pages - Users page shows cards instead of table on mobile. Language switcher persists preference. ISSUE FOUND: Categories page multi-language fields (name_en, name_pt, name_es) are NOT rendered in the UI dialog and backend create endpoint doesn't save them.

critical.issue: Multi-language fields (name_en, name_pt, name_es) are NOT saved when creating categories. The create_category_l1 function manually constructs the document and omits these fields. Line ~1093-1103 in server.py

minor.issue: Same issue - multi-language fields likely not saved (needs verification)

ui_bugs.issue: Multi-language name fields (name_en, name_pt, name_es) are defined in formData but NOT rendered as input fields in the Add/Edit Category dialog. Users cannot enter translations for category names.

passed_tests: Login page translations - English: 'Welcome Back', Portuguese: 'Bem-vindo de Volta', Spanish: 'Bienvenido de Nuevo'
Dashboard translations - Title, subtitle, KPI cards, Recent Orders section all translate correctly
Dashboard sidebar translations - All navigation items translate (Painel/Panel, Usuários/Usuarios, etc.)
Teams page translations - Title 'Equipes/Equipos', Add button 'Adicionar Equipe/Agregar Equipo'
Users page translations - Title, table headers, buttons all translate correctly
Mobile responsiveness - Dashboard KPI cards display in 2-column grid
Mobile responsiveness - Users page shows 9 user cards instead of table (md:hidden class working)
Mobile responsiveness - Teams page stats stack vertically
Language switcher persistence - Navigating between pages keeps selected language
Backend categories API returns multi-language fields in response (name_en, name_pt, name_es)
action_items: FIX BACKEND: Update create_category_l1 function (line ~1093) to include name_en, name_pt, name_es fields when creating category document
FIX BACKEND: Update create_category_l2 function similarly
FIX FRONTEND: Add input fields for name_en, name_pt, name_es in Categories.js dialog (lines 426-509) with language labels/flags
critical_code_review_comments: Backend server.py line ~1093-1103: create_category_l1 manually constructs document but omits name_en, name_pt, name_es fields that are in CategoryL1Create model
Frontend Categories.js: formData has name_en, name_pt, name_es but dialog form only renders single 'Name' input field
The getCategoryName helper function in Categories.js is implemented but won't work until multi-language fields are actually saved
context_for_next_testing_agent: i18n translations verified working on Dashboard, Teams, Users pages. Mobile responsiveness working. Categories page has multi-language fields in backend models and frontend formData but: 1) Backend create endpoints don't save them, 2) Frontend dialog doesn't render input fields for them. Test credentials: admin@redribbonops.com / Fmtvvl171**. SMTP is MOCKED.

dashboard_translations.english: Working - Dashboard - Admin, Overview of all orders
dashboard_translations.portuguese: Working - Painel - Admin, Visão geral de todos os pedidos
dashboard_translations.spanish: Working - Panel - Admin, Vista general de todos los pedidos

users_translations.english: Working - Users, Add User, table headers

mobile_responsiveness.users: Working - Shows 9 user cards instead of table (md:hidden)

categories_multilang.frontend_dialog: NOT WORKING - input fields not rendered

categories_multilang_not_working.root_cause: Backend create_category_l1 function manually constructs document dict without including name_en, name_pt, name_es fields. Frontend Categories.js has formData with these fields but dialog form doesn't render input elements for them.
reproduction_steps: 4. Notice only single 'Name' field - no multi-language inputs
5. Even if you POST with name_en/pt/es via API, they return as null
categories_multilang_not_working.mitigation: Update backend create_category_l1 to include: 'name_en': category_data.name_en, 'name_pt': category_data.name_pt, 'name_es': category_data.name_es. Update frontend dialog to render 3 additional input fields with language labels.
